---
name: EPIC
about: Multi-issue umbrella tracking a larger initiative (>=2 child issues required)
title: 'EPIC: <noun-phrase describing the initiative>'
labels: type/feature
---

<!--
Per dynolabs-io/workflow BACKLOG-STANDARDS:
- Title MUST start with `EPIC:`.
- EPIC MUST have ≥2 child issues linked under `## Child issues` at creation.
  Hollow EPICs are forbidden — split into child issues first.
- An EPIC should produce a screenshot-evidenced operator walk within ≤7 days
  when staffed full-time. If it can't, it's likely two EPICs.
- Apply ONE `status/*` label when claimed.
-->

## Problem

<1-3 sentences: what initiative this umbrella tracks. WHY it exists.
 If founder-direct directive, quote the directive verbatim + date.>

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] <high-level outcome the EPIC delivers — usually maps to founder-walk scope>
- [ ] All child issues closed with operator-walk evidence
- [ ] Screenshot of the integrated walk attached as a comment

## Out of scope

- <what this EPIC explicitly will NOT address; defer to other EPICs>

## Repos touched

- iogrid/iogrid (list every repo that will receive a commit)

## Child issues

- [ ] #<num> <one-line description>
- [ ] #<num> <one-line description>
- [ ] <add more as the EPIC unfolds — minimum 2 at creation>
